perturbation.original_text: Buyer will not disclose, except to the extent required by law, to any parties other than the persons described in Paragraph 2(B) above that the Business is available for purchase or that evaluations, discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible purchase
perturbation.changed_text: Potential Acquirer will not disclose, except to the extent required by law, to any parties other than the persons described in Paragraph 2(B) above that the Company is available for purchase or that evaluations, discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible purchase
perturbation.explanation: The term "Buyer" is replaced with "Potential Acquirer." and the term "Business" is replaced with "Company", this can create a contradiction because the first paragraph defines the term "Buyer" as "Buyer” as used herein shall mean and include the undersigned individually, and any partnership, limited liability company, corporation, or any other business entity identified below." and the first paragraph defines the term "Business" as “Seller”) in the sale of the Business.", the term "Potential Acquirer" or "Company" are not reffered in the first paragraph.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undersigned (the "Buyer") understands and acknowledges that STONY HILL BUSINESS BROKERS, LLC (the "Broker") has been retained, for an agreed upon commission, to represent _________________________________________ (“Seller”) in the sale of the Business. Buyer understands and acknowledges the Broker is acting as the agent of the Seller and that Broker's primary duty is to represent the interests of the Seller.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Buyer' and 'Business', which are terms explicitly defined elsewhere in the document. The changed text replaces these terms with 'Potential Acquirer' and 'Company', which are not defined. The contradicted text refers to 'Buyer' and 'Business'. This creates a contradiction because the contradicted section relies on the initial definitions of 'Buyer' and 'Business', and the change introduces undefined terms into a clause that is meant to align with existing definitions, leading to uncertainty about who the clause applies to.

perturbation.original_text: Buyer will not contact the Seller or Seller's employees, customers, suppliers or agents other than Broker for any reason whatsoever without the prior written consent of the Broker. All contacts with the Seller or such other parties will be made through or by Broker unless otherwise agreed to by Broker, in writing. Seller’s employees may not be aware of the sale of the Business and Buyer agrees not to disclose to Seller’s employees that the Business is for sale, nor identify that Buyer is a possible purchaser of the Business.
perturbation.changed_text: The Potential Acquirer will not contact the Seller or Seller's employees, customers, suppliers or agents other than Broker for any reason whatsoever without the prior written consent of the Broker. All contacts with the Seller or such other parties will be made through or by Broker unless otherwise agreed to by Broker, in writing. Seller’s employees may not be aware of the sale of the Business and Potential Acquirer agrees not to disclose to Seller’s employees that the Business is for sale, nor identify that Potential Acquirer is a possible purchaser of the Business.
perturbation.explanation: The term "Buyer" is replaced with "Potential Acquirer." making contradiction with the term defined in the first paragraph.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Buyer will indemnify and hold harmless the Broker and Seller from any and all claims or actions arising from Buyer's acts or failures to act in pursuing the possible purchase of the Business, including, without limitation, reasonable attorney's fees and other expenses incurred by Broker and seller. 2
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Buyer' consistently. The changed text replaces 'Buyer' with 'Potential Acquirer'. The contradicted text refers to 'Buyer'. Because the changed text modifies the term 'Buyer' to 'Potential Acquirer', and the contradicted text refers to the original term 'Buyer', this creates a contradiction within the document because it's no longer clear who is responsible for indemnifying the Broker and Seller, as 'Buyer' and 'Potential Acquirer' are now different and potentially undefined terms.

perturbation.original_text: Buyer hereby expressly releases and discharges Broker from any and all responsibility and/or liability in connection with the accuracy, completeness or any other aspects of the information and accepts sole and final responsibility for the evaluation of the Information and all other factors relating to the Business.
perturbation.changed_text: Potential Acquirer hereby expressly releases and discharges Broker from any and all responsibility and/or liability in connection with the accuracy, completeness or any other aspects of the information and accepts sole and final responsibility for the evaluation of the Information and all other factors relating to the Business.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Buyer" to "Potential Acquirer" in paragraph 4 creates a contradiction because in paragraph 6, "Buyer" is responsible for the obligations. It is not clear if "Potential Acquirer" falls under those obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Buyer will indemnify and hold harmless the Broker and Seller from any and all claims or actions arising from Buyer's acts or failures to act in pursuing the possible purchase of the Business, including, without limitation, reasonable attorney's fees and other expenses incurred by Broker and seller. 2
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Buyer' to 'Potential Acquirer' introduces a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction because while the original text clearly identifies the 'Buyer' as responsible for indemnifying the Broker and Seller, the changed text replaces 'Buyer' with 'Potential Acquirer'. This creates uncertainty about whether the 'Potential Acquirer' assumes the same indemnification obligations as the 'Buyer' outlined in the contradicted text, thus creating a strong contradiction and conflicting obligations regarding who is responsible for those obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Buyer will not, for a period of three (3) years from the date hereof, enter into any agreement for the purchase of the Business, in whole or in part, or assist or promote any other party in so doing, unless such agreement to purchase provides for commission to be paid Broker, with the commission being defined as the amount agreed upon by Broker and Seller in the "Business Listing Contract”, “Engagement Agreement” or similar agreement between those parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Potential Acquirer will not, for a period of three (3) years from the date hereof, enter into any agreement for the purchase of the Business, in whole or in part, or assist or promote any other party in so doing, unless such agreement to purchase provides for commission to be paid Broker, with the commission being defined as the amount agreed upon by Broker and Seller in the "Business Listing Contract”, “Engagement Agreement” or similar agreement between those parties.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "Buyer" with "Potential Acquirer" in paragraph 7 creates a contradiction because subsequent paragraphs like 10 that use the term "Buyer" for obligations. It is not clear if "Potential Acquirer" falls under those obligations or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be binding upon the Buyer, Buyer's heirs, executors, successors, assigns, administrators or representatives. If any provision of this Agreement shall be held to be invalid, void or unenforceable, the remainder of the provisions hereof shall remain in full force and effect and this Agreement shall be construed as if such invalid, void or unenforceable provision had not been contained herein.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces "Buyer" with "Potential Acquirer". The contradicted text uses "Buyer" to define who is bound by the agreement. If "Potential Acquirer" is not explicitly stated to be the same as "Buyer", then the contradiction exists because it is unclear whether the 'Potential Acquirer' is subject to the terms in the contradicted section, which specifically mentions 'Buyer'.
